MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Philip Soderquist, Esq. (415-374-8500) and Robert Schwartz, Esq. (415-374-8500) for Plaintiff/opposing party Andrea Steiner.
Cheryl S. Chang, Esq. (424-239-3472) for Defendant/moving party Bank of New York Mellon, as Trustee.
Josh Bryan, Esq. (916-912-4700) for Defendant/moving party Bank of New York Mellon.
Court Reporter: Alesia Collins, CSR#7551, 916-379-5553, Reported. (501)

The Court calls the matter at 9:45 am. Defendant's Counsel reports Counsel for Bayview Loan Servicing is running late. 10:05 am, matter is recalled. The Court announces the tentative ruling. All sides ARGUED. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows

DEFENDANT BAYVIEW LOAN SERVICING, LLC, BANK OF NEW YORK MELLON, NOT IN ITS INDIVIDUAL MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Alternatively Summary Adjudication is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion is GRANTED as to Bayview Loan Servicing, LLC re: causes of action for promissory estoppel, negligent misrepresentation and intentional misrepresentation (conceded by Plaintiff). Motion is otherwise DENIED as there are triable issues of material fact not entitling the moving party to a judgment as a matter of law. (Code Civ. Proc., 437c(c); Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826.) The majority of the 79 facts cited by the Defendants in support of the summary adjudication as to each cause of action is disputed. There are triable issues of fact as to the terms of the agreement. Specifically, Plaintiff and Bank of America ("BANA") had an oral agreement on the terms of payment on January 22, 2010, which Plaintiff understood would become permanent at the end of the three month period, subject to a review of financials. BANA sent Plaintiff a Special Forbearance Agreement ("SFA") with a notice date of January 22, 2010, which Plaintiff amended to reflect her oral agreement with BANA. Plaintiff wrote to BANA to complain of the discrepancies on February 5, 2010, but received no response. In reliance on the oral agreement and lack of response from BANA to the executed and returned writing, Plaintiff performed at the reduced rate for three months, at which time she received a letter stating that she was current on her loan payments. On May 10, 2010, Plaintiff again wrote to BANA to inquire whether she should continue payments per the existing agreement, but received no response. Plaintiff continued making monthly payments at the reduced rate until August 2011.

Further, Bank of New York Mellon failed to present facts or law to support its contention that it is not liable for the acts of its servicer, BANA.

Additionally, Plaintiff's first cause of action is not time barred because she asserts a breach of contract for events after 2009. Plaintiff's seventh through eleventh causes of action are not time barred as the underlying facts relate back to the original filing of the complaint. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes)

Prevailing party to submit order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Defendant submits a copy of evidentiary objections in open Court. Judge: A. James Robertson, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Alesia Collins, CSR#7551, 916-379-5553, Reported. (501)
